title: Standard Chartered Becomes First Global Bank to Offer Direct USDC Access to Institutions
summary: Standard Chartered is the first Global Systemically Important Bank authorized to let institutions mint and redeem Circle's USDC stablecoin directly, marking a major step in bridging traditional finance with digital assets.
body:
Standard Chartered has become the first Global Systemically Important Bank (G-SIB) authorized to let institutions mint and redeem USDC, the widely used stablecoin issued by Circle. This approval marks a significant step in bridging traditional finance with the crypto world, as institutions can now directly interact with USDC without intermediaries.

The bank's authorization comes as part of its efforts to integrate digital assets into its services. USDC, pegged 1:1 to the US dollar, is one of the most trusted stablecoins in the market, often used for trading, payments, and DeFi applications. This move could streamline processes for large investors and corporations looking to use stablecoins in their operations.

For everyday people, this development might not have an immediate impact, but it signals growing acceptance of crypto in traditional banking. As more institutions adopt stablecoins, we could see faster, cheaper cross-border transactions and more integration of digital assets into everyday financial services. This could eventually lead to broader adoption of crypto-related products for retail customers.

Watch for other major banks to follow suit, as this could set a precedent for wider institutional adoption of stablecoins. If you're an institution looking to explore crypto, this might be a sign that traditional finance is becoming more open to digital assets. Read more → https://decrypt.co/372674/standard-chartered-first-global-bank-direct-usdc-access
